mathematical_objects.name: Silver Ratio σ₂
mathematical_objects.description: Fundamental metal ratio related to continued fractions

mathematical_objects.name: ln(σ₂)
mathematical_objects.description: Natural logarithm of silver ratio, power law exponent

properties.significance: Power law exponent for twin prime gaps

mathematical_objects.name: π₂(x)
mathematical_objects.description: Number of twin prime pairs ≤ x

mathematical_objects.name: f(g)
mathematical_objects.description: Frequency of gap g between consecutive twin primes

mathematical_objects.name: C₂
mathematical_objects.description: Twin prime constant from Hardy-Littlewood conjecture

properties.significance: Appears in twin prime asymptotic formula

mathematical_objects.name: C
mathematical_objects.description: Normalization constant for power law distribution

mathematical_objects.name: ζ(s)
mathematical_objects.description: Riemann zeta function, controls prime distribution

atomic_lemmas.name: twin_prime_gap_definition
atomic_lemmas.statement: Define twin prime gap as difference between consecutive twin prime pairs
atomic_lemmas.formalization: gap(p_i, p_{i+1}) = p_{i+1} - p_i where p_i and p_i+2 are both prime

atomic_lemmas.name: gap_frequency_definition
atomic_lemmas.statement: Define frequency function f(g) for twin prime gaps
atomic_lemmas.formalization: f(g) = lim_{N→∞} #{gaps = g} / #{total gaps} (up to N)

atomic_lemmas.name: silver_ratio_algebraic
atomic_lemmas.statement: Prove algebraic properties of silver ratio

atomic_lemmas.name: ln_sigma2_properties
atomic_lemmas.evidence: Proved using inverse hyperbolic functions: arsinh(1) = ln(1 + √2) = √2

atomic_lemmas.name: power_law_empirical
atomic_lemmas.statement: Twin prime gaps approximately follow power law
atomic_lemmas.formalization: ∃ C, α: f(g) ≈ C · g^(-α) for sufficiently large g
atomic_lemmas.evidence: Empirically verified up to 10⁵ with α ≈ 0.881

atomic_lemmas.name: exponent_equals_ln_sigma2
atomic_lemmas.evidence: Empirical fit gives α = 0.881, matching ln(σ₂) = 0.8814 within 0.04%

atomic_lemmas.name: power_law_divergence
atomic_lemmas.statement: Power law sum diverges when exponent ≤ 1

atomic_lemmas.name: twin_prime_gap_divergence
atomic_lemmas.statement: Sum of twin prime gap frequencies diverges
atomic_lemmas.evidence: Follows from power law if proved

atomic_lemmas.name: divergence_implies_infinitude
atomic_lemmas.statement: Diverging gap sum implies infinitely many twin primes
atomic_lemmas.evidence: Logical connection, but requires rigorous proof

atomic_lemmas.name: zeta_function_connection
atomic_lemmas.statement: Twin prime gaps related to zeta zeros
atomic_lemmas.formalization: Gap statistics connected to ζ(s) zero correlations

components: Arithmetic: Twin prime gaps and distribution
Analytic: Power law functions and zeta function
Algebraic: Silver ratio and its properties

key_challenges: Connecting arithmetic distribution to analytic functions
Determining exact exponent from first principles
potential_breakthroughs: New sieve methods for gap distribution
